content.text:
<user_query>
what skills do you have here?
</user_query>

Here are the skills available in this workspace, grouped by area:

---

### **Daily standup & team status**
- **daily-standup-insights** — Builds daily standup insights by linking Azure DevOps work items with Git commits. Use when you ask for team status, standup report, or team pulse.

---

### **PR stack & Charcoal (Azure DevOps + Git)**
- **stack-create** — Create a new stacked branch (and worktree) for PR stacking with Charcoal. Triggers: “create worktree”, “stack a branch”, “create branch on top of”, etc.
- **stack-status** — Show current PR stack and branch hierarchy (including worktrees). Triggers: “show stack”, “stack status”, “list worktrees”, etc.
- **stack-pr** — Create a Pull Request in Azure DevOps for the current (or given) branch. Triggers: “create PR”, “open PR”, “submit PR”, etc.
- **stack-navigate** — Move between stacked branches with Charcoal (e.g. `gt up`/`down`), including worktrees. Triggers: “go up”, “go down”, “parent branch”, “child branch”, etc.
- **stack-update** — Update the stack after a base branch is merged (restack). Triggers: “restack”, “update stack”, “rebase stack”, “sync stack”, etc.
- **stack-merge** — Complete a PR merge in Azure DevOps and update the stack. Triggers: “merge PR”, “complete PR”, “ship PR”, etc.
- **stack-doctor** — Check stack health and surface issues. Triggers: “check stack”, “stack health”, “diagnose stack”, “fix stack”, etc.

---

### **Cursor & rules**
- **create-rule** — Create or edit Cursor rules (e.g. `.cursor/rules/`, `AGENTS.md`, coding standards, project conventions).
- **create-skill** — Walk through creating a new Agent Skill for Cursor (structure, best practices, SKILL.md).
- **update-cursor-settings** — Change Cursor/VSCode user settings (e.g. `settings.json`, themes, format on save, keybindings).

---

### **Codex (skills & docs)**
- **skill-creator** — Guide for designing and writing effective skills (new or updated).
- **skill-installer** — Install Codex skills from a list or from a GitHub repo into `$CODEX_HOME/skills`.
- **openai-docs** — Use when you need up-to-date OpenAI product/API docs, model choice, or upgrade guidance (e.g. GPT-5.4).

---

Your `.cursorrules` also define **git worktree conventions** (e.g. `.trees/<description>`, branch names like `feature/…`, `fix/…`) and point to the **Daily Standup Insights** skill under `ai/skills/daily-standup-insights/`.

If you tell me what you want to do (e.g. “create a worktree”, “show stack”, “team standup”, “create a rule”), I can use the right skill and walk you through it.
